care.summary: Spearmint (Mentha spicata) is a perennial aromatic herb prized for its refreshing, sweet-minty flavor. Easy to grow but aggressively spreading via underground runners — best grown in containers to prevent garden takeover. Thrives in moist soil with partial to full sun. Excellent for teas, culinary use, and cocktails.
care.light: Partial sun to full sun (4-6 hours daily). Tolerates partial shade well, especially in hot climates where afternoon shade prevents scorching. Indoors, place near a bright east or south-facing window.
care.water: Keep soil consistently moist but not waterlogged. Water when top inch of soil feels dry — typically every 1-2 days in warm weather, every 3-4 days in cooler months. Mulch around plants to retain moisture and cool roots. Never let soil dry completely.
care.soil: Rich, moist, well-draining soil with pH 6.0-7.5. Tolerates a wide range of soil types but thrives in loamy soil amended with compost. In containers, use a high-quality potting mix with added perlite for drainage.
care.humidity: Moderate humidity (50-70%) preferred. Tolerates lower humidity but may develop crispy leaf edges. Mist occasionally indoors or place on a pebble tray. Avoid high humidity which promotes fungal diseases.
care.fertilizer: Light feeder. Apply balanced liquid fertilizer (10-10-10) diluted to half-strength every 4-6 weeks during growing season. Avoid over-fertilizing — reduces essential oil concentration and flavor. Compost top-dressing in spring is sufficient for garden plants.
care.toxicity: Non-toxic to humans and pets. Safe for culinary use in all quantities. Mint essential oil is concentrated and should be used cautiously around pets (esp. cats) if applied topically.
common_issues: Rusty orange spots on leaves (mint rust) - fungal disease; remove affected leaves, improve air circulation, apply copper fungicide if severe
Powdery white coating (powdery mildew) - high humidity + poor airflow; space plants, water at soil level not on leaves
Yellowing leaves - overwatering or poor drainage; ensure pot has drainage holes, reduce watering frequency
Leggy, sparse growth - insufficient light; move to sunnier location and pinch back stems to encourage bushiness
Aphids or spider mites - spray with strong water jet or insecticidal soap; introduce ladybugs as biological control
Mint root borers or flea beetles - rotate plantings, use row covers, destroy heavily infested plants
Aggressive spreading via runners - plant in containers or use root barriers 12+ inches deep in garden beds
tips: ALWAYS grow in containers or raised beds with root barriers — spearmint runners will invade and choke neighboring plants
Pinch off flower buds as they form to extend leaf harvest and prevent self-seeding
Harvest sprigs anytime by cutting stems just above a leaf node — regular harvesting encourages bushier growth
Propagate easily from stem cuttings in water (roots in 5-7 days) or by dividing established root clumps in spring
Dry leaves by hanging stems upside down in a dark, well-ventilated area; freeze fresh leaves in ice cubes for drinks
Companion plant with tomatoes and cabbage (deters pests) but keep away from other herbs to prevent cross-pollination
Bring container plants indoors before first frost; spearmint is hardy but indoor plants provide year-round harvest
